memory.summary: I'm tired and stressed, but I need to finish the analysis first. My friends are inviting me to lunch, so I'll join them later.

desires: Finish the analysis and reading before 20:00 so I don't look incompetent to the professor later.
Get through this lunch quickly so I can get back to my desk without anyone asking me why I'm distracted.
Not feel like a total idiot tomorrow when I have to explain what I didn't do while at dinner.

goals.description: Finish the lunch with cohort_3 quickly, then immediately start the assignment analysis and reading.
goals.rationale: Social friction (being asked why I'm distracted) is a high cost; academic output before 20:00 is non-negotiable to avoid looking incompetent. I need to clear the social debt first so I can focus on the work without interruption.

goals.description: Complete the full analysis and reading by 19:45 to ensure buffer time before the deadline.
goals.rationale: This was my original plan from 13:00, but the incoming chat request changes the sequence. Since I have no prior goals, this is now the immediate next step after finishing lunch.

goals.description: Review tomorrow's explanation strategy for what happened at dinner.
goals.rationale: Low priority because it requires future planning energy I don't have right now. Better to just get the work done and hope the narrative holds up without overthinking the 'idiot' scenario until tomorrow.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing lunch now avoids social friction, which is high cost. I'll eat for 30 mins then immediately switch to work on assignment_value and reading_value before the deadline. This protects my academic output while keeping the group happy.
